items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Nuggets 116, Warriors 93
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Nuggets 116, Warriors 93 — Nikola Jokic led the way with a 25-point, 15-rebound double-double and Jamal Murray chipped in 20 points to help the Denver Nuggets beat the Golden State Warriors 116-93.

items.headline: Raptors distance themselves from play-in picture, rout dispirited Magic
items.blurb: Raptors distance themselves from play-in picture, rout dispirited Magic — In a game with significant Eastern Conference playoff implications, the Magic looked like a team fiddling through the pre-season. And the Raptors took full advantage. Michael Grange has the story after a historic beatdown in Toronto.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Thunder 111, Knicks 100
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Thunder 111, Knicks 100 — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander dropped 30 points and Jalen Williams added 22 points of his own to help the Oklahoma City Thunder beat the New York Knicks 111-100.

items.headline: Duke's Cayden Boozer Takes Full Blame For Turnover: I Ruined Our Team's Season
items.blurb: Duke's Cayden Boozer Takes Full Blame For Turnover: I Ruined Our Team's Season — Duke freshman point guard Cayden Boozer shouldered full responsibility Sunday for the turnover that directly led to UConn's game-winning buzzer-beater in the Huskies' 73-72 Elite Eight victory . With 7.5 seconds remaining and Duke protecting a two-point lead, Boozer caught an inbound pass near midcourt and attempted t

items.headline: Boston College Hires UConn Assistant Luke Murray As Head Coach
items.blurb: Boston College Hires UConn Assistant Luke Murray As Head Coach — Boston College has named UConn assistant Luke Murray its next men's basketball head coach, the school announced Thursday. Murray, widely regarded as a key architect of the Huskies' offensive system, played a central role in building back-to-back national championship teams in 2023 and 2024. "I am deeply honored and incredibly gratefu

items.headline: Rick Pitino Signs Extension With St. John's, Becomes Big East's Second-Highest-Paid Coach
items.blurb: Rick Pitino Signs Extension With St. John's, Becomes Big East's Second-Highest-Paid Coach — Rick Pitino has signed a contract extension with St. John's that adds one year through the 2029-30 season and includes a substantial pay increase, the school confirmed Sunday. The deal makes Pitino, 73, the Big East's second-highest-paid coach behind UConn's Dan Hurley, according to ESPN sources. "We're thr

items.headline: UConn's Braylon Mullins Hits 35-Foot Buzzer-Beater To Complete 19-Point Comeback, Stun Duke 73-72
items.blurb: UConn's Braylon Mullins Hits 35-Foot Buzzer-Beater To Complete 19-Point Comeback, Stun Duke 73-72 — Braylon Mullins stole a pass near midcourt and buried a 35-foot three-pointer at the buzzer Sunday, capping a stunning 19-point comeback as UConn defeated top-overall seed Duke 73-72 in the Elite Eight. The Huskies advance to the Final Four for the third time in four years. The sequence unfolded in

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Rockets 134, Pelicans 102
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Rockets 134, Pelicans 102 — Alperen Sengun had a game-high 36 points along with 13 rebounds, while Kevin Durant and Jabari Smith each dropped 20 points to help the Houston Rockets beat the New Orleans Pelicans 134-102.

items.headline: Yaxel Lendeborg Dominates As Michigan Blows Out Tennessee, Advances To First Final Four Since 2018
items.blurb: Yaxel Lendeborg Dominates As Michigan Blows Out Tennessee, Advances To First Final Four Since 2018 — Michigan dismantled Tennessee 95-62 Sunday at the United Center in Chicago, advancing to the program's first Final Four since 2018. The Wolverines, who set a single-season program wins record at 35, will face top-seeded Arizona on April 4 in Indianapolis and are 1.5 point betting favorites . Yaxel

items.headline: Tatum nets season-high as Celtics beat Hornets, clinch playoff berth
items.blurb: Tatum nets season-high as Celtics beat Hornets, clinch playoff berth — Jayson Tatum scored 32 points, Payton Pritchard added 28 points and the Boston Celtics defeated the cold-shooting Charlotte Hornets 114-99 on Sunday night for their third straight victory.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Trail Blazers 123, Wizards 88
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Trail Blazers 123, Wizards 88 — Toumani Camara dropped a game-high 23 points and Scoot Henderson added 21 points of his own to help lead the Portland Trail Blazers past the Washington Wizards 123-88.

items.headline: NBA Highlights (March 29)
items.blurb: NBA Highlights (March 29)

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Raptors 139, Magic 87
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Raptors 139, Magic 87 — RJ Barrett scored a game-high 24 points, Scottie barnes added 23 and 15 rebounds, as the Raptors demolished the Magic 139-87.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Nets 116, Kings 99
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Nets 116, Kings 99 — Ochai Agbaji led the way with 18 points off the bench, Nolan Traore dropped 17, and Drake Powell had 16 as the Brooklyn Nets snapped their 10-game losing skid with a 116-99 win over the Sacramento Kings.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Celtics 114, Hornets 99
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Celtics 114, Hornets 99 — Jayson Tatum poured in a game-high 32 points, going 5-for-10 from beyond the arc, while Payton Pritchard added 28 and Neemias Queta chipped in 17 as the Boston Celtics downed the Charlotte Hornets 114–99.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Pacers 135, Heat 118
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Pacers 135, Heat 118 — Pascal Siakam had a 30-point, 11-rebound double-double and Micah Potter chipped in 21 points off the bench to help the Indiana Pacers beat the Miami Heat 135-118.

items.headline: Raptors rip off historic unanswered run in blowout victory
items.blurb: Raptors rip off historic unanswered run in blowout victory — In a crucial showdown against the Orlando Magic on Sunday, the Raptors ripped off a historic 31-point unanswered run in the first half to take a commanding lead and etch their name into the record books.

items.headline: Raptors’ Barnes throws down vicious slam over Magic’s Bitadze
items.blurb: Raptors’ Barnes throws down vicious slam over Magic’s Bitadze — Watch as the ball is inbounded to Scottie Barnes who fakes the shot and drives to the basket before slamming down a vicious dunk over Goga Bitadze of the Orlando Magic.

items.headline: Raptors score 31 straight vs. Magic in record-breaking run
items.blurb: Raptors score 31 straight vs. Magic in record-breaking run — Watch as the Toronto Raptors go on a 31-0 run between the first and second quarters vs. the Magic, setting a record for the most consecutive unanswered points since tracking began in the 1996-97 season.

items.headline: NBA roundup: Montreal’s Mathurin leads Clippers to win over Bucks
items.blurb: NBA roundup: Montreal’s Mathurin leads Clippers to win over Bucks

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Clippers 127, Bucks 113
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Clippers 127, Bucks 113 — Bennedict Mathurin dropped a team-high 28 points off the bench and Darius Garland added a 15-point, 11-rebound double-double to help the Los Angeles Clippers beat the Milwaukee Bucks 127-113.

items.headline: Warriors Adding LeBron James 'Has Some Legs'
items.blurb: Warriors Adding LeBron James 'Has Some Legs' — The Golden State Warriors know they don't have a long time left to build a winner around Stephen Curry. That has the Warriors constantly looking for upgrades. Two seasons ago, the team added Jimmy Butler. This past trade deadline, Golden State made a run at Kawhi Leonard. Now, this offseason, the Warriors may make another run at adding LeBron James. T

items.headline: Milan Momcilovic Intends To Declare For NBA Draft
items.blurb: Milan Momcilovic Intends To Declare For NBA Draft — Iowa State junior forward Milan Momcilovic intends to declare as an Early Entry Candidate for the 2026 NBA Draft. Momcilovic's season ended in the Elite Eight of the NCAA Tournament when Iowa State lost to Tennessee. "I probably, definitely, (will) test the draft," Momcilovic told Cyclone Alert in a conversation at the Big 12 Tournament in Kansas

items.headline: Tyrese Maxey Returns To Sixers Lineup In Victory Over Hornets
items.blurb: Tyrese Maxey Returns To Sixers Lineup In Victory Over Hornets — Philadelphia 76ers guard Tyrese Maxey was itching to get back on the court after missing three weeks due to a finger injury. When he got his chance on Saturday, Maxey delivered. The sixth-year guard scored 26 points on 10-for-18 shooting, along with grabbing seven rebounds and handing out eight assists. That included seven points, fou

items.headline: Anthony Edwards Expected To Return Monday After Missing Six Games With Knee Injury
items.blurb: Anthony Edwards Expected To Return Monday After Missing Six Games With Knee Injury — Anthony Edwards is on track to return to action Monday at the Dallas Mavericks, giving the Minnesota Timberwolves a needed boost as the playoff race tightens in the Western Conference. Edwards was cleared Friday for all on-court basketball activities and must complete a workout Sunday and shootaround Monday before

items.headline: Jalen Brunson: Scores 32 in loss
items.blurb: Jalen Brunson: Scores 32 in loss — Brunson logged 32 points (13-22 3Pt, 4-7 3Pt, 2-4 FT), five rebounds and five assists in 36 minutes during Sunday's 111-100 loss to the Thunder. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Miles McBride: Doesn't return Sunday
items.blurb: Miles McBride: Doesn't return Sunday — McBride (undisclosed) exited Sunday's 111-100 loss to the Thunder in the third quarter and did not return, finishing with zero points (0-3 FG, 0-3 3Pt), one rebound, one assist and one block in 11 minutes. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: All 20 NBA Playoff, Play-In Teams Determined After Bucks Eliminated In Loss to Spurs
items.blurb: All 20 NBA Playoff, Play-In Teams Determined After Bucks Eliminated In Loss to Spurs — All 20 teams competing in the NBA playoffs and play-in tournament have been determined after the Milwaukee Bucks were officially eliminated from contention Saturday, though seeding remains unsettled across both conferences. The NBA regular season ends in two weeks on April 12th. Milwaukee's exit came via a 127-9

items.headline: Arizona Freshman Trio Combines For 52 Points, Secure First Final Four Since 2001
items.blurb: Arizona Freshman Trio Combines For 52 Points, Secure First Final Four Since 2001 — Top-seeded Arizona ended a 24-year Final Four drought Saturday, defeating second-seeded Purdue 79-64 at SAP Center in San Jose to advance to Indianapolis. The victory gave the Wildcats their 36th win of the season, a program record surpassing the mark set by the 1988 squad under legendary coach Lute Olson. Arizona's

items.headline: Keaton Wagler Scores 25 As Illinois Beats Iowa To Reach First Final Four Since 2005
items.blurb: Keaton Wagler Scores 25 As Illinois Beats Iowa To Reach First Final Four Since 2005 — Illinois erased a 12-2 early deficit Saturday to defeat Iowa 71-59 in the Elite Eight at Toyota Center in Houston, advancing to the program's first Final Four since 2005 when they eventually lost to North Carolina in the national championship. Freshman Keaton Wagler led the Illini with 25 points, and a suffocatin

items.headline: Alperen Sengun: Full stat line in win
items.blurb: Alperen Sengun: Full stat line in win — Sengun supplied 36 points (12-23 FG, 5-7 3Pt, 7-8 FT), 13 rebounds, seven assists, three steals and three blocks across 32 minutes in Sunday's 134-102 win over the Pelicans. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Peyton Watson: Starting Sunday
items.blurb: Peyton Watson: Starting Sunday — Watson will start Sunday versus the Warriors. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Miles McBride: Exits to locker room
items.blurb: Miles McBride: Exits to locker room — McBride exited to the locker room in the third quarter of Sunday's game against the Thunder due to an apparent groin issue, Stefan Bondy of The New York Post reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Nuclear option should get serious consideration from NBA to combat tanking
items.blurb: Nuclear option should get serious consideration from NBA to combat tanking — Try as the NBA might, but tanking isn't going away. While pondering how to de-incentivize losing, commissioner Adam Silver must consider the nuclear option.

items.headline: Paul George plays hero for 76ers in second game after suspension
items.blurb: Paul George plays hero for 76ers in second game after suspension — In only his second game since serving a 25-game suspension for violating the NBA's anti-drug policy, Paul George played the role of hero for the Philadelphia 76ers in their win over the Charlotte Hornets on Saturday night.

items.headline: Doc Rivers Said Health Issues, Lack Of Depth, Giannis Antetokounmpo Rumors Undid Bucks Season
items.blurb: Doc Rivers Said Health Issues, Lack Of Depth, Giannis Antetokounmpo Rumors Undid Bucks Season — The Milwaukee Bucks were officially eliminated from postseason contention after their home loss to the San Antonio Spurs on Saturday. Following the games, Bucks coach Doc Rivers was asked about being eliminated and the reasons for the down season for Milwaukee. "It's been disappointing obviously," River

items.headline: NBA suspends Lakers’ Doncic one game after 16th technical foul of season
items.blurb: NBA suspends Lakers’ Doncic one game after 16th technical foul of season — The Los Angeles Lakers superstar has been suspended one game by the NBA after receiving his 16th technical foul of the regular season.

items.headline: 76ers’ Tyrese Maxey returns from 10-game absence vs. Hornets
items.blurb: 76ers’ Tyrese Maxey returns from 10-game absence vs. Hornets — All-star guard Tyrese Maxey is returning to the 76ers lineup after missing 10 consecutive games due to a finger injury, the team announced on Saturday.

items.headline: Adama Bal, Grizzlies Sign 10 Day Contract
items.blurb: Adama Bal, Grizzlies Sign 10 Day Contract — The Memphis Grizzlies have signed forward Adama Bal to a hardship 10 Day contract. Memphis ruled several players out for the season due to injuries. In addition, the Grizzlies have other players dealing with various non-season-ending injuries. Bal has spent the season with the Westchester Knicks of the G League after going undrafted out of Santa Clara at

items.headline: Doc Rivers Unsure If Kevin Porter Jr Will Play Again This Season
items.blurb: Doc Rivers Unsure If Kevin Porter Jr Will Play Again This Season — Milwaukee Bucks guard Kevin Porter Jr's season may be coming to an early end. Porter is dealing with synovitis in his right knee. Bucks head coach Doc Rivers said he'd be "surprised" if Porter plays again this season. Milwaukee went into play on Saturday in 11th place in the Eastern Conference. The Bucks are nine games behind the M
